researched_category.notes: Pinterest is a visual discovery and bookmarking platform. Its API v5 covers content management (Pins, Boards), ads management, analytics, catalogs, and conversion tracking.

one_liner.value: Pinterest API v5 allows developers to programmatically manage Pins, Boards, ad campaigns, catalogs, analytics, and conversions on the Pinterest platform.
one_liner.notes: The API covers content creation/management, ads, analytics, catalogs, targeting, and conversion tracking.

methods.notes: Pinterest uses OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code Grant). Access tokens are sent as Bearer tokens in the Authorization header. App registration provides Client ID and Client Secret. There is also a client_credentials grant type mentioned for some endpoints.

access.notes: Trial access is self-serve free after creating a business account and registering an app. However, pins/boards created in Trial mode are sandbox-only (not publicly visible). Upgrading to Standard access (required for production use) requires submitting a video demo of the OAuth flow and API calls for manual review. Standard access is free but gated by this review. An additional Marketing Developer Partners (MDP) program exists for large-scale partners but is curated/invite-only.

summary.value: Pinterest API v5 is a well-documented REST API with OpenAPI 3.0.3 specification published on GitHub. It covers: Pins (CRUD), Boards (CRUD), User accounts, Ad accounts, Campaigns, Ad Groups, Ads, Catalogs/Feeds/Product Groups, Analytics (multiple levels), Targeting (audiences, customer lists, keywords), Conversions API, Media uploads, Trends. Rate limits are documented per category. An official Python SDK exists for campaign management. The API also supports webhooks for conversions.
summary.notes: OpenAPI spec available at github.com/pinterest/api-description. Official Python SDK supports ad campaign management. Webhook support for conversion events.

agent_interface.notes: Pinterest launched an official first-party MCP server on June 17, 2026 (alpha, named partners only, read-only for ad data). Multiple community MCP servers exist on GitHub (collactivelabs/pinterest-mcp-server, terryso/mcp-pinterest, CDataSoftware/pinterest-mcp-server-by-cdata). An official Python SDK is available. Pinterest internally uses MCP extensively for their own AI agents.

rationale.value: The Pinterest API v5 is fully documented, has a public REST API with OpenAPI spec, supports OAuth 2.0, and has both official and community MCP servers. However, building a production agent toolkit faces two key gates: (1) Standard access (required for public pin/board visibility and higher rate limits) requires a manual video demo review by Pinterest, with no guaranteed timeline; (2) The official MCP server is in alpha with named partners only. For sandbox/development use, Trial access is freely available. For production agent toolkits targeting end users, the Standard access approval process is the main blocker.
rationale.notes: Trial access is easy to get but limited. Standard access is free but requires manual review with video demo. The official MCP server is partner-gated. Community MCP servers exist but require Pinterest API credentials.

s1.quote: One API to seamlessly integrate with Pinterest. Securely plan content, manage ads, upload product feeds, and more.
s1.title: Pinterest Developers - Welcome

s2.quote: The Pinterest API significantly increases the scope of what is possible for you to create as a site or application developer. Access to the Pinterest API can be used for: Conversion tracking, Content management, Ad management, Targeting, Shopping management, Analytics & Reporting.
s2.title: Pinterest REST API 5.30.0

s3.quote: Pinterest API services are only available to authenticated and authorized users. Make sure your API requests on a user's behalf include a current access token... OAuth prerequisites
s3.title: Set up authentication and authorization

s4.quote: Access to Pinterest APIs via User Authorization requires following a flow based on OAuth 2.0.
s4.title: pinterest/api-quickstart

s5.quote: If approved for API use, your app receives Trial access, which is best for exploring our API features. If you want to offer Pinterest capabilities to your users, we recommend upgrading to Standard access... Pins and Boards created with Trial access are only visible to their creator as Sandbox entities.
s5.title: Understanding our access tiers

s6.quote: Both the Trial and Standard tiers cost nothing... The three things that actually gate you are non-monetary: the Trial-to-Standard video-demo review, the per-category rate ceilings, and a data-storage rule.
s6.title: Pinterest API Pricing: Complete Guide for 2026

s7.quote: Pinterest requires a video demo (OAuth flow + main Pinterest actions) to upgrade from Trial -> Standard.
s7.title: Pinterest API (Trial -> Standard). Any success stories / tips for Standard access approval?

s8.quote: To upgrade to Standard access, you must first have an approved app with Trial Access. The upgrade process involves submitting a request that includes a video demonstration of your app's functionality.
s8.title: Upgrading from trial to standard - Pinterest Business Community

s9.quote: This repository contains OpenAPI (version 3.0.3) descriptions for Pinterest's REST API.
s9.title: pinterest/api-description

s10.quote: The Pinterest SDK currently offers a Python library that supports campaign management and simplifies authentication and error handling.
s10.title: Pinterest SDK

s11.quote: Trial access: 1000 requests per day for all API requests. Standard access: 100 requests per second per user per app for all API requests.
s11.title: Rate limits

s12.quote: On June 17, 2026, Pinterest and Microsoft Advertising both shipped official Model Context Protocol servers... Pinterest's MCP server is in alpha with named partners only and gives AI agents read access to campaign performance data.
s12.title: Pinterest & Microsoft Launch Ad MCP Servers for AI

s13.quote: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for integrating the Pinterest API with AI agents and LLMs.
s13.title: collactivelabs/pinterest-mcp-server

s14.quote: CData's Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server for Pinterest. This read-only MCP Server allows you to connect to Pinterest data from Claude Desktop through CData JDBC Drivers.
s14.title: pinterest-mcp-server-by-cdata

limitations: The Pinterest developer portal pages (developers.pinterest.com) load heavily with JavaScript and fetched content was often minimal/boilerplate-only. Secondary sources (Blotato, Digital Applied, community forums) were used to supplement.
The official MCP server is in alpha with named partners only - no public endpoint URL is available yet.
Standard access approval timeline is not published and varies.
